element_introduction_order.mastery_context: player controls single commander on map
recruiting soldiers, pikemen, cavalry to form platoon
each unit moves once per player turn
units within commander radius gain attack/defense bonuses
fliers strong vs soldiers, cavalry strong vs soldiers, soldiers strong vs pikemen
treat command recovers 3 HP, troops auto-recover near commander
coordinating multiple squads against scores of enemies

rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: scenario-based progression with turn-based tactical breathing room between major engagements

difficulty_curve.early_game: tutorial scenarios establishing unit hiring and basic turn mechanics with small unit counts
difficulty_curve.mid_game: expansion to larger battlefields with 30+ units requiring command range strategy and affinity awareness
difficulty_curve.late_game: massive encounters against score-level enemy forces demanding multi-squad coordination
notable_spikes: transition from single squad to multi-unit battles
scenarios requiring optimal commander positioning for radius bonuses

notable_level_patterns.description: each scenario is self-contained narrative battle revealing story progression through combat interaction
player must hire appropriate unit types (soldiers, pikemen, cavalry, fliers) to counter enemy composition using affinity advantages
tactical depth emerges from maintaining units within command radius to receive attack/defense bonuses while managing movement economy
series distinguishes itself with 30+ controllable units in large battles, departing from smaller-scale tactical RPGs of the era
